revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''職員令'''（しきいんりょう）は、[[律令|令]]の篇目の1つ。[[養老律令|養老令]]では第2番目に位置していており、…」
revision.text:
'''職員令'''（しきいんりょう）は、[[律令|令]]の篇目の1つ。[[養老律令|養老令]]では第2番目に位置していており、全80条からなる。『[[大宝律令|大宝令]]』では'''官員令'''と称されていた。
== 概要 == 唐の[[開元]]7年令（[[719年]]）の三師三公台省職員令、寺監職員令、衛府職員令、州県鎮戍嶽涜関津（ちんじゅがくとくかんしん）職員令に相当し、これらを1つに統合したものになっている。
[[二官八省]]とその被管の二職・十六寮・三十司・[[弾正台]]、[[五衛府]]と被管の一司、左右[[馬寮]]、左右兵庫、内兵庫、左右[[京職]]と被管二司、[[摂津職]]、[[大宰府]]、[[諸国]]、[[郡]]、[[軍団 (古代日本)|軍団]]、国博士、医師について、官職名、員数、職掌を定めている。
職員構成は、官司ごとに[[四等官]]・[[品官]]、[[雑任]]（[[史生]]・[[舎人]]・使部・[[伴部]]など）、仕丁・[[衛士]]・[[品部]]・[[雑戸]]などからなると規定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 『角川第二版日本史辞典』p427、[[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]：編、[[角川書店]]、1966 * 『岩波日本史辞典』p520、監修：[[永原慶二]]、[[岩波書店]]、1999年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の官制]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しきいんりよう}} [[Category:日本の令]]

contributor.username: ja>壁掛成蛸は三考する葦である

revision.text:
'''職員令'''（しきいんりょう）は、[[律令|令]]の篇目の1つ。[[養老律令|養老令]]では第2番目に位置しており、全80条からなる。『[[大宝律令|大宝令]]』では'''官員令'''と称されていた。
== 概要 == 唐の[[開元]]7年令（[[719年]]）の三師三公台省職員令、寺監職員令、衛府職員令、州県鎮戍嶽涜関津（ちんじゅがくとくかんしん）職員令に相当し、これらを1つに統合したものになっている。
[[二官八省]]とその被管の二職・十六寮・三十司・[[弾正台]]、[[五衛府]]と被管の一司、左右[[馬寮]]、左右兵庫、内兵庫、左右[[京職]]と被管二司、[[摂津職]]、[[大宰府]]、[[諸国]]、[[郡]]、[[軍団 (古代日本)|軍団]]、国博士、医師について、官職名、員数、職掌を定めている。
職員構成は、官司ごとに[[四等官]]・[[品官]]、[[雑任]]（[[史生]]・[[舎人]]・使部・[[伴部]]など）、仕丁・[[衛士]]・[[品部]]・[[雑戸]]などからなると規定されている。

== 年表 == * 明治2（1869）年7月、職員令（官制改定職員令ヲ頒ツ）が公布される<ref name=shokuin>{{Cite |和書|title=官制改定職員令ヲ頒ツ |date=明治2年07月08日 |id=太00015100 |author=太政官|url=https://www.digital.archives.go.jp/item/1341702 |publisher=}国立公文書館デジタルアーカイブ}}</ref>。 * 明治4（1871）年7月、太政官職制が公布される<ref>{{Cite |和書|title=太政官職制沿革原文 |date=1871 |publisher=国立国会図書館 |doi=10.11501/3860359}}</ref>
{{節stub}}

{{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しきいんりよう}} [[Category:日本の令]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令). It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7年令, [[719]]) of Tang China..."
revision.text:
{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7年令, [[719]]) of [[Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[Dazaifu]], [[provinces]] (諸国), [[districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * "KADOKAWA Second Edition Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 427, edited by [[Mitsuhisa Takayanagi]] and [[Risō Takeuchi]], [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1966. * "Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 520, supervised by [[Keiji Nagahara]], [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1999.
== See Also == * [[Japanese Administrative System]]
{{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese Ritsuryō Codes]] [[ja:職員令]]

{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7年令, [[719]]) of [[Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[Dazaifu]], [[provinces]] (諸国), [[districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * "KADOKAWA Second Edition Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 427, edited by [[Mitsuhisa Takayanagi]] and [[Risō Takeuchi]], [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1966. * "Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 520, supervised by [[Keiji Nagahara]], [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1999.
== See Also == * [[Japanese Administrative System]]
{{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Japanese Ritsuryō Codes]] [[ja:職員令]]

The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[Dazaifu]], [[provinces]] (諸国), [[districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).

The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[Dazaifu]], [[provinces]] (諸国), [[districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).

{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7年令, [[719]]) of [[Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[Dazaifu]], [[provinces]] (諸国), [[districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * "KADOKAWA Second Edition Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 427, edited by [[Mitsuhisa Takayanagi]] and [[Risō Takeuchi]], [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1966. * "Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 520, supervised by [[Keiji Nagahara]], [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1999.
== See Also == * [[Japanese Administrative System]]

{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7年令, [[719]]) of [[Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[Dazaifu]], [[provinces]] (諸国), [[districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * "KADOKAWA Second Edition Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 427, edited by [[Mitsuhisa Takayanagi]] and [[Risō Takeuchi]], [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1966. * "Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 520, supervised by [[Keiji Nagahara]], [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1999.
== See Also == * [[Japanese Administrative System]]

{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).

The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[Dazaifu]], [[provinces]] (諸国), [[districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7年令, [[719]]) of [[Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[Dazaifu]], [[provinces]] (諸国), [[districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * "KADOKAWA Second Edition Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 427, edited by [[Mitsuhisa Takayanagi]] and [[Risō Takeuchi]], [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1966. * "Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 520, supervised by [[Keiji Nagahara]], [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1999.== See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Administrative System|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system}}

'''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).

The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.

The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Hinkan|Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).

'''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).

It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7th year 令, [[:en:719|719]]) of [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.

The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]], [[:en:Provinces|Provinces]] (諸国), [[:en:districts|districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.

The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Hinkan|Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).

== Notes ==
{{reflist}}

In the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7th year 令, [[:en:719|719]]) of [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]], [[:en:Provinces|Provinces]] (諸国), [[:en:districts|districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Hinkan|Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).
== Notes == {{reflist}}

{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}}
{{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}}
'''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).

It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7th year 令, [[:en:719|719]]) of [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.

The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]], [[:en:Provinces|Provinces]] (諸国), [[:en:districts|districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.

The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Hinkan|Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).

== Notes ==
{{reflist}}

The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.

The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. {{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}}
{{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}}
'''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).

It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7th year 令, [[:en:719|719]]) of [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.

The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]], [[:en:Provinces|Provinces]] (諸国), [[:en:districts|districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.

The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Hinkan|Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).

== Notes ==
{{reflist}}

== References ==
* "KADOKAWA Second Edition Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 427, edited by [[Mitsuhisa Takayanagi]] and [[Risō Takeuchi]], [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1966.
* "Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 520, supervised by [[Keiji Nagahara]], [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1999.

In the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7th year 令, [[:en:719|719]]) of [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), [[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]], [[:en:Provinces|Provinces]] (諸国), [[:en:districts|districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Hinkan|Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * "KADOKAWA Second Edition Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 427, edited by [[Mitsuhisa Takayanagi]] and [[Risō Takeuchi]], [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1966. * "Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 520, supervised by [[Keiji Nagahara]], [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Administrative System|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Administrative System|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}}
{{japanese-history-stub}}

{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}} (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}} (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7th year 令, {{ill|719|en|719|WD=Q23537|lt=719}}) of [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}, [[:en:Provinces|Provinces]] (諸国), [[:en:districts|districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Hinkan|Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).

{{for|the proclamations issued by the Meiji government|職員令 (太政官制)}} {{Cite_sources|date=April 2024}} '''Shikiinryō''' (職員令) is one of the chapters of the [[Ritsuryō]] code. In the {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}} (養老令), it is positioned as the second chapter and consists of 80 articles. In the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}} (大宝令), it was referred to as the '''Kan'inryō''' (官員令).
It corresponds to the Kaigen 7th Year Code (開元7th year 令, {{ill|719|en|719|WD=Q23537|lt=719|1=719}}) of [[:en:Tang China|Tang China]], which included the "Three Teachers and Three Dukes Ministry and Provincial Personnel Code," the "Temple and Supervision Personnel Code," the "Guard Office Personnel Code," and the "Provincial, County, Garrison, Mountain, River, Pass, and Port Personnel Code." The Shikiinryō consolidates these various codes into a unified system.
The chapter defines the official titles, personnel numbers, and duties for the [[Nikkan Hasshō]] (二官八省) and their subordinate organizations, including the Two Departments, Sixteen Bureaus, Thirty Offices, [[Danjō-dai]] (弾正台), and the [[Goeifu]] (五衛府) with its subordinate office. It also includes the Left and Right [[Umaryo]] (馬寮), Left and Right Armories, Inner Armory, Left and Right [[Kyōshoku]] (京職) with its two subordinate offices, [[Settsu Province Office]] (摂津職), {{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu}}, [[:en:Provinces|Provinces]] (諸国), [[:en:districts|districts]] (郡), [[military corps]] (軍団), and positions such as National Scholars and Physicians.
The personnel structure is regulated for each government office, specifying [[Shitōkan|Four-Tier Officials]] (四等官), [[Hinkan|Pinkan]] (品官), [[Zatsunin]] (雑任) (including [[Shōsei]] (史生), [[Toneri]] (舎人), attendants, and [[Tomonomiyatsuko|Tomo no Miyatsuko]] (伴部)), laborers (仕丁), guards (衛士), professional guilds (品部), and miscellaneous households (雑戸).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * "KADOKAWA Second Edition Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 427, edited by [[Mitsuhisa Takayanagi]] and [[Risō Takeuchi]], {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1966. * "Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary," p. 520, supervised by [[Keiji Nagahara]], {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Japanese Administrative System|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Administrative System|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}}
